spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS DEC Special Graphics; issue #12
notes: DEC corner glyphs fold to ASCII plus signs on the IIe.

spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS DEC Special Graphics; issue #12
notes: DEC horizontal line q folds to ASCII hyphen.

spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS DEC Special Graphics; issue #12
notes: DEC vertical line x folds to ASCII vertical bar.

spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS DEC Special Graphics; issue #12
notes: A small ACS box renders as the documented ASCII +-| approximation.

spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS DEC Special Graphics; issue #12
notes: The DEC cross and tee glyphs all fold to ASCII plus signs.

spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS DEC Special Graphics; issue #12
notes: Unmapped bytes in the selected DEC graphics set pass through unchanged.

spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS DEC Special Graphics; issue #12
notes: Designate DEC graphics, print folded glyphs, then reset G0 to ASCII.

spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS DEC Special Graphics; issue #12
notes: After ESC(B, q/x/l/m render as ordinary ASCII letters again.

spec_ref: VT100-UG: SCS G0/G1, SO/SI locking shift; issues #12, #52
notes: G0 and G1 are designated independently: ESC(0 makes G0 DEC special graphics while ESC)B keeps G1 at ASCII. With GL on G0, q folds to '-'; SO invokes G1 (ASCII) so q renders as 'q'; SI returns to G0 so q folds to '-' again. The row '-q-' fails unless the two designations are tracked separately AND SO/SI switch the active set.
